title: Add receive-side verification to testnet_shielded_payment_end_to_end
description:
wallet/tests/e2e_testnet.rs test broadcasts Alice's tx but never verifies Bob received funds. Add: (1) poll loop waiting for tx to be mined (poll client.latest_height() until height > broadcast_block_height, max 5 min); (2) scan Bob's wallet with SaplingIvkDecryptor; (3) assert bob_store.balance_with_confirmations(1).await confirmed_zatoshi >= PAYMENT_AMOUNT_ZATOSHI.

Algorithm:
1. After send_payment() returns txid, record broadcast_height = client.latest_height().await.
2. Poll loop: every 30s, call client.latest_height(). When height > broadcast_height, break. Timeout after 300s (half the overall 600s budget) with hard failure.
3. Construct Bob scanner: SaplingIvkDecryptor::new(&bob_sk.to_dfvk().ivk_bytes()), CompactBlockScanner::new(bob_client, bob_store.clone(), decryptor). Call scanner.load_state() then scanner.scan_to_tip().
4. Assert bob_store.balance_with_confirmations(1).await?.confirmed_zatoshi >= PAYMENT_AMOUNT_ZATOSHI.
5. Assert bob_store.spendable_notes(0).await? is non-empty (proves IVK decryption worked end-to-end).

Defensive rules: wrap each await in proper error propagation; use tokio::time::timeout for both the mining wait and the Bob scan; never log bob_seed bytes; client.latest_height() is fallible, handle Err with retry.
acceptance_criteria: Test compiles; Bob wallet scan + balance assertion present in source; cargo test -p nie-wallet succeeds
